response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 20+ years of experience and genuine strengths in distributed systems, engineering practices, and developer tooling, but he is a poor match for this specific role. The most critical gap is C++: the job requires 8 years of C++ experience, yet the resume shows only a brief stint at National Instruments (roughly 6 months in 2007-2008) and a bachelor thesis project as C++ touchpoints. His primary languages throughout his career are TypeScript, Node.js, DLang, and JavaScript. Beyond C++, the role demands deep cloud compute platform and large-scale infrastructure ownership, whereas Bogdan's background is predominantly product engineering and web-stack work. His leadership experience, while present in Staff Engineer titles and community workshops, does not demonstrate the cloud/DevOps team mentorship the role targets.
strengths: Demonstrated distributed systems and high-throughput experience at Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests/second, low-latency async I/O)
Strong engineering-culture track record: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, community workshops, and open-source contributions
Long, continuous career across embedded, mobile, web, and infrastructure contexts showing broad adaptability
concerns: Effectively zero C++ experience relative to the 8-year minimum requirement — a non-negotiable disqualifier for this role
No cloud compute platform or large-scale infrastructure ownership; AWS CDK usage at Optilyz is the only cloud reference and is shallow
No documented technical leadership of cloud or DevOps engineers; Staff Engineer titles cover product/web work, not infrastructure teams
